title: URL Shortener
desc: Create short URLs with click tracking and analytics
what_you_build: Create short URLs with click tracking and analytics
what_you_learn: How to use Flask in a real project

steps.title: Setup & Plan
steps.desc: Create project folder, initialize git, plan features.
steps.code:
mkdir url-shortener
cd url-shortener
git init
touch main.py README.md
steps.hint: Plan before coding! Write down 3-5 features you want to build.

steps.title: Build Core Features
steps.desc: Implement the main functionality of URL Shortener.
steps.code:
# Start with the simplest version
# Add features one by one
# Test each feature before moving on
steps.hint: Build the minimum working version first, then add features.

steps.title: Add Error Handling
steps.desc: Handle edge cases and invalid inputs.
steps.hint: Think: what could go wrong? Handle each case.

steps.title: Test & Polish
steps.desc: Test with different inputs, fix bugs, clean up code.
steps.code:
# Test with:
# - Normal inputs
# - Edge cases (empty, zero, negative)
# - Invalid inputs
steps.hint: Test with inputs you DON'T expect, not just the happy path.

steps.title: Push to GitHub
steps.desc: Write README and push your project to GitHub.
steps.code:
git add .
git commit -m 'feat: complete URL Shortener'
git push origin main
steps.hint: A good README makes your project stand out to recruiters!

extensions: Add a web interface with Flask
